get.description: List captain threads for a project.

schema.description: Filter to threads created by this Capy user ID. Mutually exclusive with authorEmail.
Filter to threads created by the organization member with this email address. Returns an empty page if the email does not match an organization member. Mutually exclusive with authorId.
Filter to threads this Capy user ID authored or participated in. Mutually exclusive with participantEmail.

Filter to threads the organization member with this email address authored or participated in. Returns an empty page if the email does not match an organization member. Mutually exclusive with participantId.
Filter by thread origin source.
Filter by the thread's rolled-up pull request state. Combines with prNumber and branch.
Filter to threads with this tag name (lowercase).
Filter to threads linked to this Slack channel ID (e.g. C0123456789). Combines with slackThreadTs.
Free-text search across thread titles and content.

post.description: Create a new captain thread and immediately start execution, optionally assigning existing project tags.

get.description: Get a single captain thread.

post.description: Stop an actively running captain thread.
Archive a captain thread. If the thread is actively running, the archive succeeds first and then the API attempts to stop the active run on a best-effort basis.
Unarchive a captain thread. This does not restart or resume execution.

get.description: Fetch the current session identity token for a captain thread. The caller must have access to the project that owns the thread. The same token is also written inside the captain VM to `/etc/capy/session-token` with permissions `0600`, so code running inside the VM can read it locally. A `410` response means the session has already ended. A `422` response with code `session_not_ready` means the token has not been minted yet during initial bootstrap; retry with backoff.

post.description: Send a message to an existing thread.

get.description: List messages in a captain thread.

put.description: Replace the tags assigned to a captain thread. Every tag must already be defined for the project's organization.

get.description: Get a task by UUID.
Get the current diff for a task by UUID.
List projects available to the API token.
Get a single project.
List reusable captain-thread tags defined for the project's organization.

post.description: Create a reusable captain-thread tag for the project's organization. Reusing an existing tag name restores it and updates its color.

get.description: List models available to the API.
Get organization usage totals, user breakdowns, and the paginated thread activity timeline shown in the usage dashboard. Monetary amounts are decimal US dollar numbers.

schema.description: Comma-separated project IDs to include.
Filter usage to build, captain, or review activity.

get.description: Get Setup used for future project runs. Setup contains VM size, three automatic repository scripts, opt-in Commands, terminals, previews, and pre/post tool hooks. Environment variables are configured separately.

patch.description: Save Setup for future project runs. The update becomes active immediately; secret and environment variable fields are not accepted.

schema.description: Set to true to confirm that saving Setup replaces active deprecated .capy/settings.json hooks for future runs.

get.description: Get whether optional Snapshots are enabled for the project. Snapshots are independent from Setup.

patch.description: Enable or disable optional Snapshots. This does not gate saving or testing Setup.

get.description: Get warm pool configuration, pool status counts, and whether warm pool is enabled for the project's organization.

patch.description: Update warm pool configuration. Supports partial updates — only include fields to change.

post.description: Trigger a test boot to verify setup commands work. Optionally pass setupCommands to save them before booting. Returns an instance ID to poll for results. Test VMs are auto-deleted after 15 minutes.

get.description: Get status and provisioning logs for a warm pool instance. Use this to poll test boot results. The response includes a summary with the failed step and error message.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Warm pool instance ID (returned by test boot).

get.description: List recent warm pool instances with their status. Useful for monitoring pool health.

post.description: Delete all active warm pool instances (VMs and DB records). Optionally trigger replenishment to refill the pool with fresh instances.

get.description: List browser snapshots for a project. Private snapshots are only visible to their creator.

post.description: Create a new browser snapshot with cookies and localStorage state. Domains are automatically extracted from cookie domains.

get.description: Get a browser snapshot by ID including its full storage state.

patch.description: Update browser snapshot metadata. Only the snapshot creator can change the isPrivate flag.

get.description: List names and configuration metadata for the effective actor's personal project environment variables. Secret values are never returned. Admin service users may explicitly target a same-organization project member with impersonateUserEmail.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

put.description: Bulk-upsert personal project environment variables for the effective actor. Omitted variables are preserved and secret values are never returned. Admin service users may explicitly target a same-organization project member with impersonateUserEmail.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

delete.description: Soft-delete one personal project environment variable for the effective actor. The name path segment must be URL encoded. Admin service users may explicitly target a same-organization project member with impersonateUserEmail.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

get.description: List project automations visible to the effective actor. Creator-scoped automations are private to their creator. Admin service users may explicitly target a same-organization project member with impersonateUserEmail.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

post.description: Create an automation owned by the effective actor. Admin service users may create on behalf of a same-organization project member only with the explicit impersonateUserEmail query parameter.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

get.description: Get an automation visible to the effective actor. Creator-scoped automations are private to their creator.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

patch.description: Partially update an automation visible to the effective actor. Creator-private ownership is enforced.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

delete.description: Soft-delete an automation visible to the effective actor. Creator-private ownership is enforced.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

post.description: Run an automation now as its immutable creator. The caller must be able to view the automation; service tokens never silently inherit a human identity.

schema.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

post.description: Verify a session identity token issued for a captain session. The caller must have access to the project that owns the session so the endpoint cannot be used for cross-tenant enumeration. Failure responses are intentionally opaque: only `expired` is distinguished from `invalid`, and callers should always confirm the returned `projectId` matches the project they expect before trusting the result.

authorId.description: Filter to threads created by this Capy user ID. Mutually exclusive with authorEmail.

authorEmail.description: Filter to threads created by the organization member with this email address. Returns an empty page if the email does not match an organization member. Mutually exclusive with authorId.

participantId.description: Filter to threads this Capy user ID authored or participated in. Mutually exclusive with participantEmail.

participantEmail.description: Filter to threads the organization member with this email address authored or participated in. Returns an empty page if the email does not match an organization member. Mutually exclusive with participantId.

prState.description: Filter by the thread's rolled-up pull request state. Combines with prNumber and branch.

tag.description: Filter to threads with this tag name (lowercase).

slackChannelId.description: Filter to threads linked to this Slack channel ID (e.g. C0123456789). Combines with slackThreadTs.

q.description: Free-text search across thread titles and content.

status.description: Coarse Captain run-loop status retained for compatibility. Prefer runState/waitingOn/blockedOn for completion and waiting UX.

runState.description: Full computed execution state for the agent thread.

waitingOn.description: Async system dependencies expected to continue without user action.

blockedOn.description: User/integration gates required before progress can continue.

pendingWakeups.description: Pending append-and-resume notifications waiting to be processed.

status.description: Coarse Captain run-loop status retained for compatibility. Prefer runState/waitingOn/blockedOn for completion and waiting UX.

runState.description: Full computed execution state for the agent thread.

waitingOn.description: Async system dependencies expected to continue without user action.

blockedOn.description: User/integration gates required before progress can continue.

pendingWakeups.description: Pending append-and-resume notifications waiting to be processed.

token.description: Opaque session identity token. This value is a JWT and is typically 700-1000 bytes long.

agentType.description: Filter usage to build, captain, or review activity.

agents.description: Which agent types this applies to. Omit to apply to all agents (default: all)

pre.description: Commands to run BEFORE a tool executes. Keys are tool names (bash_run, edit, write, etc.), values are arrays of shell commands or objects with commands and agents filter

agents.description: Which agent types this applies to. Omit to apply to all agents (default: all)

post.description: Commands to run AFTER a tool executes. Keys are tool names, values are arrays of shell commands or objects with commands and agents filter. Can use ${variable} interpolation from tool args/results

maxOutputLength.description: Maximum characters of hook output to include in context (default: 2000)

truncateStrategy.description: How to truncate output exceeding maxOutputLength: 'head' keeps start, 'tail' keeps end, 'middle' keeps both ends

agents.description: Which agent types this applies to. Omit to apply to all agents (default: all)

pre.description: Commands to run BEFORE a tool executes. Keys are tool names (bash_run, edit, write, etc.), values are arrays of shell commands or objects with commands and agents filter

agents.description: Which agent types this applies to. Omit to apply to all agents (default: all)

post.description: Commands to run AFTER a tool executes. Keys are tool names, values are arrays of shell commands or objects with commands and agents filter. Can use ${variable} interpolation from tool args/results

maxOutputLength.description: Maximum characters of hook output to include in context (default: 2000)

truncateStrategy.description: How to truncate output exceeding maxOutputLength: 'head' keeps start, 'tail' keeps end, 'middle' keeps both ends

agents.description: Which agent types this applies to. Omit to apply to all agents (default: all)

pre.description: Commands to run BEFORE a tool executes. Keys are tool names (bash_run, edit, write, etc.), values are arrays of shell commands or objects with commands and agents filter

agents.description: Which agent types this applies to. Omit to apply to all agents (default: all)

post.description: Commands to run AFTER a tool executes. Keys are tool names, values are arrays of shell commands or objects with commands and agents filter. Can use ${variable} interpolation from tool args/results

maxOutputLength.description: Maximum characters of hook output to include in context (default: 2000)

truncateStrategy.description: How to truncate output exceeding maxOutputLength: 'head' keeps start, 'tail' keeps end, 'middle' keeps both ends

replaceLegacyHooks.description: Set to true to confirm that saving Setup replaces active deprecated .capy/settings.json hooks for future runs.

name.description: Shell environment variable name, limited to 191 characters by storage.

impersonateUserEmail.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

webhookUrl.description: Relative bearer-capability URL for incoming-webhook automations. Treat this value as sensitive because anyone who possesses it can trigger the automation.

impersonateUserEmail.description: Admin service users may explicitly act as this same-organization project member. Human and non-admin service-user tokens must omit this parameter.

webhookUrl.description: Relative bearer-capability URL for incoming-webhook automations. Treat this value as sensitive because anyone who possesses it can trigger the automation.
